name: CVF Engineering Test-Driven Development
owner: CVF ASSF registry and external absorption governance surface
purpose: Guide failing-test-first implementation, bug reproduction, regression coverage, and proof that behavior works before closure.
useWhen: Use when a governed CVF task matches the upstream `test-driven-development` skill pattern and needs CVF-owned test-first guidance after explicit runtime package-loader selection.
doNotUseWhen: Do not use as autonomous runtime authority, external CLI/MCP authority, provider/live proof, public-sync proof, test execution authority, commit permission, or permission to bypass active CVF work-order, source-verification, session-sync, or commit-steward gates.
inputs: operator request or governed task context
active CVF authority and allowed-scope boundaries
outputs: next governed action if promotion is needed
executionConstraints: ACTIVE production package generated from AGSK-R2/R3/R6/R7 evidence and ASCP-P1-P3 productionization; production executor may request receipt-backed package use only through CVF runtime adapters; no filesystem, git, browser, provider routing, public-sync, or action authority is granted by package loading alone
acceptanceEvidence: AGSK-R2 review artifact, AGSK-R3 package-root worker return, AGSK-R6 pilot promotion pattern, AGSK-R7 batch promotion, runtime package-loader body-read smoke, ASSF certified metadata admission checker, ASSF anatomy checker, and generated skill-index drift check; ASCP-P1-P3 production executor tests, CLI/MCP wrapper tests, ACTIVE source promotion, generated index drift check, and live E2E proof
authorityCeiling: bounded advisory test-first guidance only; loading this entry or package body cannot authorize implementation, test execution, commit, browser, network, provider, public, or production action beyond the active governed work order
sideEffects: none from metadata loading; any future use that edits files, runs tools, installs dependencies, opens browsers, uses providers, or affects production requires separate authorization
permissions: read this metadata, cited CVF-governed artifacts, and the pinned local source mirror only
rollback: restore this registry entry to the AGSK-R3 PROPOSED state, restore the paired package root metadata, then regenerate docs/reference/agent_system_skills/generated/skill-index.json
safeStop: if ACTIVE source checks, receipt checks, provider diagnostics, or governed work-order authority are missing, stop and return a denied execution packet with source-truth trace
capabilityBoundary: package root ACTIVE at docs/reference/agent_system_skills/packages/cvf-engineering-test-driven-development/ for production-scoped receipt-backed package execution; production executor and CLI/MCP wrapper are implemented; no automatic invocation, filesystem/git/browser action authority, provider router mutation, public-sync, or production action beyond the active governed work order
resolverBehavior: metadata-only; resolver may return identity, trigger, risk, and source fields only, and must not treat APPROVED package metadata as automatic invocation or ACTIVE runtime authority
loaderBoundary: loading this metadata or package body never grants authority; production execution requires ACTIVE source state, a skill usage receipt, activation policy receipt, package use-proof receipt, and production execution receipt
externalMutationBoundary: external CLI/MCP wrapper may return receipt-backed package execution envelopes only; no external mutation, daemon behavior, public API, provider routing, public-sync, commit, or merge authority is permitted
platformCompatibility: cross-platform metadata; upstream skill content may contain tool-specific assumptions that require future normalization before activation
shellAssumptions: no shell execution required for metadata-only candidate
osConstraints: none for metadata; future package bodies must record platform constraints explicitly
